title: Monthly Budget Balance
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: When you look at your own month, do you usually end with surplus, balance, or shortage?
What is one concrete step you can take next month to move closer to a balanced or surplus budget?

stages.prompt: Which situation shows a healthier monthly budget?

options.label: Income ₹20,000, expenses ₹19,000, ₹1,000 kept aside
options.reflection: Correct. Even a small surplus, if repeated, builds a cushion and reduces the need for future borrowing.

options.label: Income ₹20,000, expenses also ₹20,000 with no savings plan
options.reflection: Breaking even is better than a loss, but with no savings, any shock can immediately push you into debt.

options.label: Income ₹20,000, expenses ₹22,000 every month
options.reflection: A regular shortage usually leads to accumulating debt or unpaid bills. This is a warning sign.

options.label: No idea about exact income or expenses
options.reflection: Not knowing numbers makes it very hard to feel in control or improve your situation.

stages.prompt: Which habit helps you maintain a balanced budget over many months?

options.label: Reviewing and adjusting the budget at the end of each month
options.reflection: Yes. A monthly review helps you learn from mistakes and correct your plan for the next month.

options.label: Ignoring issues as long as basic bills are somehow paid
options.reflection: Ignoring small problems often lets them grow into big ones.

options.label: Relying only on hope that income will keep increasing
options.reflection: Hope is not a plan. You still need to manage current income carefully.

options.label: Using more and more credit whenever there is a shortage
options.reflection: Relying on credit to fill every gap can quickly lead to a dangerous debt cycle.
